mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly lifting valuation multiples and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.5 percentage points within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off positioning, depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of industrial production and housing starts indicates a synchronized demand-supply shock that compresses corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 101.5 and HOUST falls below 1450
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve model R-squared by at least 0.02 over single-proxy baselines, or forward returns in the joint contraction regime are not statistically lower than the baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, rising real rates driven by Fed policy tighten financial conditions faster than nominal easing, suppressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns in high-inflation periods do not exhibit a statistically significant negative correlation with rising real rates, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP per quarter reflects expanding aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth decelerates while SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of observations, invalidating the demand-returns linkage.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment growth exceeds 250,000 for two consecutive months while the unemployment rate remains below 4.0%, wage-push inflation pressures accelerate, prompting aggressive Fed tightening expectations that compress equity multiples and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint labor market tightness signal fails to predict negative forward returns with > 55% accuracy, or SPX forward returns are not significantly lower than the unconditional mean during triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production rises while real retail sales fall indicates supply chain bottlenecks outpacing consumer demand, eroding profit margins and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term does not significantly improve out-of-sample prediction accuracy, or forward returns in the divergence regime are not statistically lower than periods of aligned supply-demand growth.

mechanism_narrative: During a Fed tightening cycle, a sudden spike in the unemployment rate signals an unexpected demand shock that forces policy pivot expectations, rapidly compressing discount rates and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising for 3 consecutive months and UNRATE rises > 0.4pp in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not exhibit a positive response to the policy pivot signal, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for VIX levels, indicating the signal captures volatility rather than fundamental valuation shifts.
